one_line_definition: Matrix relating outgoing modal pressure amplitudes to incoming modal pressure amplitudes at both ends of a duct element; encodes reflection and transmission for all propagating modes
Ratio (in dB) of incoming to outgoing acoustic power across a duct element, derived from eigenvalues of S'†S'
Empirical expressions for the characteristic impedance Z_c and propagation constant k_c of a fibrous porous material as a function of frequency and flow resistivity
Semi-empirical model for the acoustic impedance of a perforated plate accounting for hole geometry and grazing flow effects
Sound-absorbing wall treatment in a duct composed of a perforated face-sheet, porous layer, and rigid backing; characterized by its surface impedance
Technique that describes wave propagation in each layer by a matrix relating boundary quantities (stress, displacement or pressure waves) at the two surfaces, cascading matrices across all layers
Matrix method that assembles all layer equations into one large system simultaneously, avoiding numerical instability of the Transfer Matrix method at high frequency-thickness products

aliases_seen: phase velocity vs. frequency, wavenumber dispersion
one_line_definition: Plots of phase velocity (or wavenumber) versus frequency for guided wave modes in a plate or layer; roots of the characteristic equation of the multilayer system
Guided elastic waves propagating in a plate with traction-free surfaces; split into symmetric and antisymmetric families characterized by their dispersion curves
Guided plate waves that radiate (leak) energy into adjacent fluid or solid media, resulting in exponential amplitude decay along the propagation direction
Decomposition of the wave field inside each layer into individual upward- and downward-traveling bulk (P, SV, SH) wave components whose amplitudes are treated as unknowns
Theory of wave propagation in fluid-saturated porous solids predicting two compressional waves (fast and slow) and one shear wave
Compressional wave in a porous medium where frame and fluid move out of phase; highly attenuated at low frequencies
Compressional wave in a porous medium where frame and fluid move in phase; less attenuated than the slow wave
Coefficient accounting for all wave paths (direct plus all internal multiple-reflection orders) from transmitter to receiver through a porous slab
Coefficient summing all reflected paths (including those returning after internal reflections) back toward the transmitter
Determination of unknown material parameters (porosity, tortuosity, permeability) by fitting modeled transmission/reflection coefficients to experimental measurements
2×2 matrix relating forward and reverse pressure wave amplitudes on one face of a layer to those on the opposite face, used for acoustic channel modeling
System that conveys electrical power or data across a solid barrier using ultrasonic waves via piezoelectric transducers bonded to each side
Two-port model (one electrical: V, I; one mechanical: p⁺, p⁻) of a piezoelectric disk incorporating its coupling coefficient h₃₃ and backing reflection coefficient
Two-port network representation that maps input voltage/current to output voltage/current; used to connect piezoelectric transducer models to electronic networks
Amplitude attenuation of an ultrasonic beam due to geometric divergence away from a piston source; modeled as a layer-specific correction in the transfer matrix stack
Product of medium density and sound speed (ρc); governs the reflection and transmission of waves at interfaces between different media

aliases_seen: full nonlinear Euler equations for fluctuations
one_line_definition: Conservation of mass and momentum equations for inviscid flow, decomposed into mean flow and fluctuating parts, used to propagate acoustic disturbances on a CFD background flow
Two-step aeroacoustics approach: CFD computes unsteady mean flow; a separate acoustic solver (CAA) propagates sound on that flow field
Logarithmic measure of RMS acoustic pressure relative to a reference (20 µPa in air); expressed in dB
Numerical boundary treatment that allows outgoing acoustic waves to leave the computational domain without spurious reflections
Hybrid uRANS/LES turbulence model that resolves turbulent structures in unstable flow regions while falling back to RANS elsewhere; used as CFD input for EAP
Branch of CFD focused on the prediction and propagation of aerodynamically generated sound
